notes: Knockout predictions calibrated to provided group outcomes and Elo snapshot; extra-time only used when a winner emerges by 120'.

roundOf32.reasoning: Canada’s stronger underlying level and group-stage goal difference (+5) should translate into more consistent chance creation than South Africa’s more reactive approach.
Germany’s group-winning form (+6 GD) and talent depth should overwhelm a Paraguay side that qualified as a best third with a negative differential.
Morocco are well-organized, but the Netherlands’ balance and finishing edge from wide areas should be decisive in a tight game.
Japan’s press can disrupt, but Brazil’s individual quality in the final third and transition threat should create the higher-quality chances.
France’s perfect group (9 points, +8) and elite pace/finishing should punish Sweden if they defend deep and concede territory.
Norway’s attacking focal point and higher Elo-level control should tilt a close matchup against an Ivorian side that can be stretched in transition.
Ecuador’s physicality and tempo should keep it level, but Mexico’s tournament momentum (9 points, +6) and home-region comfort can carry them through a shootout.
England’s set-piece and box efficiency should be too much for DR Congo, who are likely to spend long spells defending their own third.
With strong group results (+4 GD) and host-region edge, the USA should generate more transition opportunities than Bosnia can comfortably handle.
Senegal’s athletic press can neutralize Belgium for long stretches, but Belgium’s experience and composure give them a narrow edge in penalties.
Croatia’s control can slow the game, but Portugal’s shot volume and depth off the bench make them slightly more likely to prevail in a shootout.
Spain’s possession structure and chance suppression should blunt Austria’s direct threats and limit them to low-quality looks.
Switzerland’s defensive organization and better game management should edge a competitive Algeria side that can be dangerous but inconsistent.
Argentina’s perfect group (9 points, +7) and technical superiority should open the game early and control it thereafter.
Colombia’s pressing-to-possession balance and higher baseline quality should limit Ghana’s transitions and produce the clearer chances.
Australia’s structure keeps it close, but Egypt’s ability to manage low-scoring games and marginally fresher legs can swing a shootout.

roundOf16.reasoning: Germany’s control in midfield and defensive reliability should reduce Canada’s transition outlets and turn territory into goals.
This profiles as a near-coinflip on quality, but Brazil’s individual match-winners can decide it late once spaces open in extra time.
Norway’s direct threat is real, yet France’s pace and ball-winning should keep them from establishing sustained pressure.
Mexico’s momentum and crowd energy help, but England’s set-piece edge and deeper attacking options should decide a tight contest.
The USA’s intensity can create moments, but Belgium’s technical quality and ability to play through pressure should produce more high-value chances.
Portugal can match Spain athletically, yet Spain’s ball control and chance prevention make them slightly more likely to survive a shootout.
Switzerland are difficult to break down, but Argentina’s attacking combinations and ability to sustain pressure should tell over 90 minutes.
Egypt’s low-block can frustrate, but Colombia’s pressing recoveries and midfield runners should create enough to win.

quarterfinals.reasoning: Germany’s structure keeps it close, but Brazil’s transition threat and one-on-one quality in wide areas can swing the decisive moments.
England can compete on set pieces and tempo, but France’s speed in behind and finishing efficiency give them the higher ceiling in open play.
Spain’s ability to pin Belgium back and limit transitions should reduce Belgium’s best route to goal and produce a narrow win.
Colombia’s press makes it uncomfortable, but Argentina’s game management and late attacking quality can decide it in extra time.

semifinals.reasoning: Both sides can create at elite rates, but France’s depth and counterpunch threat make them slightly more likely to find the extra-time winner.
Argentina can match Spain’s intensity, yet Spain’s territorial control and chance suppression increase their odds of edging a shootout.

thirdPlace.reasoning: With slightly more pace and width-driven creation, Brazil can exploit Argentina’s rotations and take third in a more open match.

final.reasoning: France’s direct threat keeps it tight, but Spain’s sustained control and depth of technical options can produce the decisive extra-time moment.
